rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE RUTH SCHNELL GRAHAM TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Respondents Cooper, White & Cooper'S Llp'S And K. E. Goofin'S Notice Of Demurrer And Demurrer To Petitioner'S Verified Petition.
rulings.Rulings: Tentative Ruling to Demurrer of Respondents Cooper, White & Cooper and Kenneth E. Goodin: 1. Petitioner cannot remove the demurrer from the court calendar. Petitioner's Supplemental Verified Petition filed on February 6, 2007 is in fact a first amended pleading. Any further amended pleading requires court approval for filing. Respondent's demurrer to Petitioner's February 6 pleading remains on the court calendar of April 19. 2. The demurrer is sustained without leave to amend. As a co-trustee acting without the consent of the other co-trustee, petitioner lacks standing to bring an action against counsel. As a beneficiary, petitioner cannot bring an action under the Probate Code against counsel for the co-trustees. Petitioner cannot create a legally unrecognized fiduciary position of "de facto trustee."
